$$
t_k
\eqdef
\frac{\hb_k-\beta_{k,0}}{\HSE{\hb_k}}
\ddist
t_{n-p}
\qquad
\text{under }H_0:\beta_k=\beta_{k,0}
\qquad
t_k^{\text{obs}}\text{ = observed }t_k
$$

$$
\text{CI}_{1-\alpha}(\beta_k)
=
\left[
\hb_k
\pm
t_{n-p}\paren{1-\frac{\alpha}{2}}
\HSE{\hb_k}
\right]
$$

Let $T_{n-p}\sim t_{n-p}$.

$$
\ba
p\text{-value}
&=
2\Pr \paren{\abs{T_{n-p}}\ge \abs{t_k^{\text{obs}}}}
\\
&=
2\paren{1-F_{t_{n-p}}\paren{\abs{t_k^{\text{obs}}}}}
\ea
$$

$$
\text{CI}_{1-\alpha}\paren{\mu\paren{\vxs}}
=
\left[
\hat{\mu}\paren{\vxs}
\pm
t_{n-p}\paren{1-\frac{\alpha}{2}}
\HSE{\hat{\mu}\paren{\vxs}}
\right]
$$

$Y^*$ denotes a new observation
(not in the training data),
with corresponding covariate pattern $\vxs$.
Let $\hat{Y}^* \eqdef \hat{\mu}\paren{\vxs}$.

$$
\text{PI}_{1-\alpha}\paren{Y^*|\vxs}
=
\left[
\hat{Y}^*
\pm
t_{n-p}\paren{1-\frac{\alpha}{2}}
\HSE{Y^*-\hat{Y}^*}
\right]
$$

$$
\HSE{Y^*-\hat{Y}^*}
=
\hs\sqrt{1 + \tp{(\vxs)}(\mX'\mX)^{-1}\vxs}
$$

$$
\Var{Y^* - \hat{Y}^*}
= \sigma^2\paren{1 + \tp{(\vxs)}(\mX'\mX)^{-1}\vxs}
$$

Let $\hat{\Sigma} \eqdef \hVar{\hb} = \hs^2(\mX'\mX)^{-1}$.

$$
\hVar{\hat{\mu}\paren{\vx}} = \tp{\vx}\hat{\Sigma}\vx
$$

Let $\Delta\mu\paren{\vx, \vxs} = \mu\paren{\vx} - \mu\paren{\vxs}$,
and let $\diffvx = \vx - \vxs$;
then:

$$
\hVar{\widehat{\Delta\mu}\paren{\vx, \vxs}} = \tp{\diffvx}\hat{\Sigma}\diffvx
$$

{{< pagebreak >}}
